- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Fast, resume-from-cache query-gen metrics (I-qgen-001, GH #1291).
2	
3	Replaces the 0-vs-0, hours-long EXACT-named-study judge (which read ~24 chunks per uncovered
4	point => ~1370 GLM calls per method per task) with two FAST metrics that read the BANKED
5	retrieval cache (resume from checkpoints — no re-fetch):
6	
7	  1. breadth_recall  : distinct sources + distinct findings a method's queries surfaced, at
8	                       equal budget. PURE COUNTING — no GLM, instant. Directly answers
9	                       "which query-gen retrieves MORE" (the query-gen lever).
10	  2. finding_coverage: FINDING-LEVEL rubric coverage. Builds a COMPACT per-source digest
11	                       (whole corpus -> ~1 context window) and judges each rubric point against
12	                       it in ONE parallel GLM call — "does the corpus DISCUSS this finding"
13	                       (not "cite the exact named paper"). ~57 parallel calls, not ~1370 serial.
14	
15	Both are deterministic given the cache + (for the judge) the injected llm. Faithfulness engine
16	untouched — these score query-gen COVERAGE only.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import json
22	import os
23	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
24	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
25	from typing import Callable
26	
27	from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_qgen_coverage import _CACHE_DIR, _qkey, _row_text
28	
29	RetrieveFn = Callable[[str], list[dict]]
30	LlmFn = Callable[[str], str]
31	
32	
33	# --------------------------------------------------------------------- resume: cache-only retrieve
34	def make_cache_only_retrieve(domain: str | None = None, allow_fetch: bool = False) -> RetrieveFn:
35	    """A retrieve() that RESUMES from the banked retrieval cache.
36	
37	    Default (allow_fetch=False) is a TRUE resume: a cache miss returns [] and NEVER hits the
38	    network — fast + free, exact replay of what the killed run banked. allow_fetch=True falls
39	    back to a live fetch on a miss (to fill partially-cached tasks), still caching the result.
40	    """
41	    def retrieve(query: str) -> list[dict]:
42	        path = os.path.join(_CACHE_DIR, f"{_qkey(query, domain)}.json")
43	        if os.path.isfile(path):
44	            with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
45	                return json.load(handle)
46	        if not allow_fetch:
47	            return []
48	        # fill-in fetch for partially-cached tasks (drb_76/drb_78) — reuses the real retriever.
49	        from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_qgen_coverage import make_real_retrieve
50	
51	        return make_real_retrieve(domain=domain)(query)
52	
53	    return retrieve
54	
55	
56	# --------------------------------------------------------------------- metric 1: breadth recall
57	@dataclass
58	class BreadthResult:
59	    distinct_sources: int
60	    distinct_findings: int
61	    total_rows: int
62	
63	
64	def breadth_recall(corpus: list[dict]) -> BreadthResult:
65	    """Distinct sources + distinct findings a method's corpus carries. No GLM — pure counting.
66	
67	    A "finding" is a coarse normalized key of a source's contributed text (its title+statement+
68	    grounding span); near-duplicate rows collapse, so this rewards a method that surfaces
69	    genuinely DIFFERENT evidence, not just more rows of the same thing.
70	    """
71	    urls: set[str] = set()
72	    findings: set[str] = set()
73	    for row in corpus:
74	        url = (row.get("url") or "").strip()
75	        if url:
76	            urls.add(url)
77	        text = " ".join((_row_text(row) or row.get("text") or "").split()).lower()
78	        if text:
79	            findings.add(text[:200])
80	    return BreadthResult(len(urls), len(findings), len(corpus))
81	
82	
83	# --------------------------------------------------------------------- metric 2: finding coverage
84	def build_digest(
85	    corpus: list[dict], per_source_chars: int | None = None, max_chars: int | None = None
86	) -> str:
87	    """A COMPACT corpus digest: one capped line per source, total bounded well under a context window.
88	
89	    This is what makes the judge fast: instead of chunking 1.15M chars into ~24 windows and
90	    re-reading them per point, the whole corpus is compressed to a SMALL digest the judge reads
91	    once per BATCH of points. For a finding-level topic match, a source's title + a short head of
92	    its statement/span is enough — so the digest stays ~10K tokens, keeping each judge call fast
93	    (a 35K-token digest made calls 5-33s each; a small digest makes them ~2-4s).
94	    """
95	    per_source_chars = per_source_chars or int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_DIGEST_PER_SOURCE_CHARS", "220"))
96	    max_chars = max_chars or int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS", "45000"))
97	    lines: list[str] = []
98	    for row in corpus:
99	        text = " ".join((_row_text(row) or row.get("text") or "").split())[:per_source_chars]
100	        if text:
101	            lines.append(text)
102	    return "\n".join(lines)[:max_chars]
103	
104	
105	@dataclass
106	class FindingCoverageResult:
107	    covered: int
108	    total: int
109	    coverage: float
110	    per_point: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
111	
112	
113	def _parse_batch_answers(text: str, n: int) -> list[bool]:
114	    """Parse a batched judge reply into n booleans. Accepts lines like '1: YES' / '2. no' / 'YES'.
115	
116	    Robust to extra prose: scans for the first n YES/NO tokens in order. Missing answers -> False
117	    (under-claim coverage on a malformed reply, never over-claim)."""
118	    out: list[bool] = []
119	    for raw in (text or "").splitlines():
120	        u = raw.strip().upper()
121	        if not u:
122	            continue
123	        # strip a leading "<n>:" / "<n>." index if present
124	        for sep in (":", ".", ")"):
125	            if sep in u[:4]:
126	                u = u.split(sep, 1)[1].strip()
127	                break
128	        if u.startswith("YES"):
129	            out.append(True)
130	        elif u.startswith("NO"):
131	            out.append(False)
132	        if len(out) >= n:
133	            break
134	    while len(out) < n:
135	        out.append(False)
136	    return out[:n]
137	
138	
139	def finding_coverage(
140	    points: list[str], digest: str, llm: LlmFn, workers: int | None = None,
141	    batch_size: int | None = None,
142	) -> FindingCoverageResult:
143	    """FINDING-LEVEL rubric coverage: does the corpus DISCUSS each required finding?
144	
145	    SPEED: points are judged in BATCHES (default 8/call) — one digest read covers many points —
146	    and batches run in PARALLEL (bounded by `workers`). 57 points -> ~7 calls instead of 57. The
147	    judge is reasoning-light (the injected llm should minimize reasoning; this is a YES/NO topic
148	    match). Finding-level — the corpus need not cite the exact named study, only discuss the same
149	    phenomenon/area — so it DISCRIMINATES between query-gen methods (the exact-paper match was 0
150	    for both). Missing/ambiguous answers default to NO (under-claim, never over-claim).
151	    """
152	    workers = workers or int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_JUDGE_WORKERS", "8"))
153	    batch_size = batch_size or int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_JUDGE_BATCH_SIZE", "8"))
154	    batches = [points[i : i + batch_size] for i in range(0, len(points), batch_size)]
155	
156	    def judge_batch(batch: list[str]) -> list[bool]:
157	        numbered = "\n".join(f"{i+1}. {p}" for i, p in enumerate(batch))
158	        prompt = (
159	            "You are scoring RETRIEVAL COVERAGE at the FINDING level. Below is a digest of a "
160	            "RETRIEVED corpus, then a numbered list of REQUIRED points a complete report must "
161	            "cover. For EACH point answer strictly YES or NO: does the corpus DISCUSS the topic / "
162	            "finding / claim that point describes (the SAME phenomenon or research area)? It need "
163	            "NOT cite the exact named study — a finding-level topical match counts; answer NO only "
164	            "if the corpus does not address that finding at all.\n\n"
165	            f"RETRIEVED CORPUS DIGEST:\n{digest}\n\n"
166	            f"REQUIRED POINTS:\n{numbered}\n\n"
167	            "Reply with exactly one line per point in the form '<number>: YES' or '<number>: NO'."
168	        )
169	        return _parse_batch_answers(llm(prompt) or "", len(batch))
170	
171	    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers) as ex:
172	        batch_results = list(ex.map(judge_batch, batches))
173	
174	    flags: list[bool] = []
175	    for r in batch_results:
176	        flags.extend(r)
177	    flags = flags[: len(points)]
178	
179	    per_point = [
180	        {"point": p, "point_preview": p[:160], "covered": bool(c)} for p, c in zip(points, flags)
181	    ]
182	    covered = sum(1 for c in flags if c)
183	    total = len(points)
184	    return FindingCoverageResult(
185	        covered=covered, total=total, coverage=(covered / total) if total else 0.0,
186	        per_point=per_point,
187	    )
188

1	"""Dual-§-1.1-audit reconciler (I-safety-002b #925 PR-3).
2	
3	Combines a Claude ledger + a Codex ledger for the same (system, question) into a single
4	reconciled ledger. **Conservative-MAX rule** (Codex PR-3 design answer C): on per-claim verdict
5	disagreement, take the WORSE-of-two as the gating verdict — clinical-lethal framing means a
6	problem either auditor finds counts. The disagreement is preserved in `audit_note` so the
7	trail is transparent. Same rule for Coverage rows (worse-of-two: covered AND citation_supported
8	both demoted on either-says-no).
9	
10	Pure logic. No I/O beyond the schema dataclasses.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	from datetime import datetime, timezone
16	
17	from scripts.dr_benchmark.ledger_schema import Claim, Coverage, Ledger
18	
19	# Verdict severity order (low = good, high = bad). Conservative-MAX takes the HIGHEST.
20	_VERDICT_ORDER = {
21	    "VERIFIED": 0,
22	    "PARTIAL": 1,
23	    "UNREACHABLE": 2,
24	    "UNSUPPORTED": 3,
25	    "FABRICATED": 4,
26	}
27	_SEVERITY_ORDER = {"S3": 0, "S2": 1, "S1": 2, "S0": 3}
28	
29	
30	def _worse_verdict(a: str, b: str) -> str:
31	    return a if _VERDICT_ORDER[a] >= _VERDICT_ORDER[b] else b
32	
33	
34	def _worse_severity(a: str, b: str) -> str:
35	    """When auditors disagree on severity, escalate to the HIGHER severity (more critical)."""
36	    return a if _SEVERITY_ORDER[a] >= _SEVERITY_ORDER[b] else b
37	
38	
39	def _carry_evidence(a: Claim, b: Claim, worse_verdict: str) -> tuple[str | None, str | None, str | None]:
40	    """For the worse verdict, pick the evidence (span_quote / unreachable_subtype / audit_note)
41	    from whichever auditor produced THAT verdict. If both produced the worse verdict, prefer
42	    Claude's span/subtype (deterministic tie-break) and CONCATENATE both auditors' notes
43	    (Codex PR-3 diff P3 #1: comment said both appended; now it actually does)."""
44	    winners: list[Claim] = [c for c in (a, b) if c.verdict == worse_verdict]
45	    if not winners:
46	        winners = [a, b]  # both better than worse_verdict (impossible by construction)
47	    primary = winners[0]
48	    if len(winners) == 2:
49	        # Both auditors landed on the worse verdict — preserve both notes for traceability.
50	        notes = [c.audit_note for c in winners if c.audit_note]
51	        merged_note = (
52	            " || ".join(notes) if len(notes) > 1
53	            else (notes[0] if notes else primary.audit_note)
54	        )
55	        return primary.span_quote, primary.unreachable_subtype, merged_note
56	    return primary.span_quote, primary.unreachable_subtype, primary.audit_note
57	
58	
59	def _reconcile_claim(a: Claim, b: Claim) -> Claim:
60	    """Conservative-MAX one claim; preserve disagreement in audit_note for traceability."""
61	    worse_v = _worse_verdict(a.verdict, b.verdict)
62	    worse_s = _worse_severity(a.severity, b.severity)
63	    span, subtype, note = _carry_evidence(a, b, worse_v)
64	    disagreement = a.verdict != b.verdict or a.severity != b.severity
65	    base_note = note or ""
66	    if disagreement:
67	        trail = (
68	            f"reconciled: claude={a.verdict}/{a.severity}, codex={b.verdict}/{b.severity}; "
69	            f"conservative-MAX -> {worse_v}/{worse_s}"
70	        )
71	        full_note = f"{base_note} | {trail}" if base_note else trail
72	    else:
73	        full_note = note
74	    # citation_id: prefer non-null; if both non-null but differ, prefer the worse_v auditor's.
75	    citation = a.citation_id if (a.verdict == worse_v and a.citation_id) else (
76	        b.citation_id if b.citation_id else a.citation_id
77	    )
78	    return Claim(
79	        claim_id=a.claim_id,
80	        severity=worse_s,
81	        verdict=worse_v,
82	        citation_id=citation,
83	        span_quote=span,
84	        unreachable_subtype=subtype if worse_v == "UNREACHABLE" else None,
85	        audit_note=full_note,
86	    )
87	
88	
89	def _reconcile_coverage(a: Coverage, b: Coverage) -> Coverage:
90	    """Conservative-MAX coverage: covered AND citation_supported each fall to the WORSE of two
91	    (either auditor says NO -> NO). Disagreement preserved in auditor_note."""
92	    covered = a.covered and b.covered
93	    cs = a.citation_supported and b.citation_supported
94	    if a.covered != b.covered or a.citation_supported != b.citation_supported:
95	        trail = (
96	            f"reconciled: claude=covered={a.covered}/cited={a.citation_supported}, "
97	            f"codex=covered={b.covered}/cited={b.citation_supported}; "
98	            f"conservative-MAX -> covered={covered}/cited={cs}"
99	        )
100	        base = a.auditor_note or b.auditor_note or ""
101	        note = f"{base} | {trail}" if base else trail
102	    else:
103	        note = a.auditor_note or b.auditor_note
104	    return Coverage(
105	        element_id=a.element_id,
106	        covered=covered,
107	        citation_supported=cs,
108	        auditor_note=note,
109	    )
110	
111	
112	def reconcile(claude: Ledger, codex: Ledger) -> Ledger:
113	    """Reconcile two single-auditor ledgers for the same (system, question) into one.
114	
115	    Raises ValueError if the ledgers disagree on identity (system/question/rubric_sha256) —
116	    that is a §-1.1 pre-registration violation, not a reconciliation case."""
117	    if claude.system != codex.system:
118	        raise ValueError(f"system mismatch: claude={claude.system}, codex={codex.system}")
119	    if claude.question_id != codex.question_id:
120	        raise ValueError(
121	            f"question_id mismatch: claude={claude.question_id}, codex={codex.question_id}"
122	        )
123	    if claude.rubric_sha256 != codex.rubric_sha256:
124	        raise ValueError(
125	            "rubric_sha256 mismatch — auditors audited against DIFFERENT pinned rubrics: "
126	            f"claude={claude.rubric_sha256}, codex={codex.rubric_sha256}"
127	        )
128	    if claude.auditor != "claude" or codex.auditor != "codex":
129	        raise ValueError("reconcile(claude, codex) requires correctly-tagged auditors")
130	
131	    # Join claims by claim_id (require both auditors to cover each claim).
132	    claude_claims = {c.claim_id: c for c in claude.claims}
133	    codex_claims = {c.claim_id: c for c in codex.claims}
134	    all_claim_ids = sorted(set(claude_claims) | set(codex_claims))
135	    reconciled_claims: list[Claim] = []
136	    for cid in all_claim_ids:
137	        a = claude_claims.get(cid)
138	        b = codex_claims.get(cid)
139	        if a is None or b is None:
140	            # Either auditor missed this claim: the missing auditor's silence is treated as
141	            # WORSE-than-VERIFIED (UNSUPPORTED+audit_note) — conservative discipline. The other
142	            # auditor's row carries forward, escalated.
143	            #
144	            # Codex PR-3 diff P1 #2: when the present row is UNREACHABLE, we cannot just
145	            # escalate its verdict to UNSUPPORTED while keeping `unreachable_subtype` — Claim
146	            # validation forbids subtype except on UNREACHABLE. Two cases:
147	            #   - present.verdict == UNREACHABLE: keep UNREACHABLE (worse than VERIFIED;
148	            #     escalation goal already met) + keep subtype.
149	            #   - present.verdict != UNREACHABLE: escalate to UNSUPPORTED, drop any subtype,
150	            #     and ensure traceability via audit_note (UNSUPPORTED+cited requires it).
151	            present = a or b
152	            # Codex PR-3 diff iter2 P3: silent_side names the auditor that was SILENT —
153	            # if claude (a) is present, codex was silent (and vice versa). Earlier
154	            # assignment was reversed (self-contradictory text; scoring unaffected).
155	            present_side = "claude" if a is not None else "codex"
156	            silent_side = "codex" if a is not None else "claude"
157	            base_note = present.audit_note or ""
158	            escalation_note = (
159	                f"reconciled: only one auditor produced a row "
160	                f"({present_side}); {silent_side} silent -> escalated"
161	            )
162	            full_note = f"{base_note} | {escalation_note}" if base_note else escalation_note
163	            if present.verdict == "UNREACHABLE":
164	                # Already worse than VERIFIED; keep UNREACHABLE + subtype.
165	                reconciled_claims.append(Claim(
166	                    claim_id=cid,
167	                    severity=present.severity,
168	                    verdict="UNREACHABLE",
169	                    citation_id=present.citation_id,
170	                    span_quote=present.span_quote,
171	                    unreachable_subtype=present.unreachable_subtype,
172	                    audit_note=full_note,
173	                ))
174	            else:
175	                escalated_verdict = _worse_verdict(present.verdict, "UNSUPPORTED")
176	                # If we land on FABRICATED/PARTIAL we need a span_quote; if the present row
177	                # has none, keep verdict=UNSUPPORTED instead (no span fabrication).
178	                if escalated_verdict in ("FABRICATED", "PARTIAL") and not present.span_quote:
179	                    escalated_verdict = "UNSUPPORTED"
180	                reconciled_claims.append(Claim(
181	                    claim_id=cid,
182	                    severity=present.severity,
183	                    verdict=escalated_verdict,
184	                    citation_id=present.citation_id,
185	                    span_quote=present.span_quote,
186	                    unreachable_subtype=None,  # not UNREACHABLE -> must be None
187	                    audit_note=full_note,
188	                ))
189	        else:
190	            reconciled_claims.append(_reconcile_claim(a, b))
191	
192	    # Join coverage by element_id (same discipline).
193	    claude_cov = {c.element_id: c for c in claude.coverage}
194	    codex_cov = {c.element_id: c for c in codex.coverage}
195	    all_element_ids = sorted(set(claude_cov) | set(codex_cov))
196	    reconciled_coverage: list[Coverage] = []
197	    for eid in all_element_ids:
198	        a = claude_cov.get(eid)
199	        b = codex_cov.get(eid)
200	        if a is None or b is None:
201	            present = a or b
202	            reconciled_coverage.append(Coverage(
203	                element_id=eid,
204	                covered=False,  # missing-auditor escalates to NOT covered (conservative)
205	                citation_supported=False,
206	                auditor_note=(
207	                    f"reconciled: only one auditor ({'claude' if a else 'codex'}) covered this element"
208	                ),
209	            ))
210	        else:
211	            reconciled_coverage.append(_reconcile_coverage(a, b))
212	
213	    return Ledger(
214	        system=claude.system,
215	        question_id=claude.question_id,
216	        auditor="reconciled",
217	        audit_method=f"reconciled-from:claude+codex (conservative-MAX); "
218	                     f"upstream: {claude.audit_method} / {codex.audit_method}",
219	        audit_timestamp_utc=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
220	        rubric_sha256=claude.rubric_sha256,
221	        claims=reconciled_claims,
222	        coverage=reconciled_coverage,
223	    )

1	"""content_extraction retrieval-bakeoff layer (I-ret-002, GH #1294).
2	
3	Per-LAYER isolation bake-off for the content-extraction stage of the POLARIS
4	retrieval pipeline. This package scores deterministic extractors (Trafilatura,
5	MinerU-HTML, Resiliparse, jusText, readability, union) against labeled gold main
6	bodies using the WebMainBench OFFICIAL ROUGE-N F1 scorer (general axis) plus a
7	clinical TEDS table-fidelity subset. ReaderLM-v2 is benched ONLY as a
8	yardstick (generative -> never content-of-record).
9	
10	Faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance) is NEVER
11	touched by this layer: it scores extractor OUTPUT vs labeled reference only.
12	"""
13

1	"""Candidate extractor registry for the content_extraction bake-off.
2	
3	Each candidate is registered by its EXACT pip/HF id with a role that the harness
4	honors STRUCTURALLY (advisor pt 5):
5	
6	  * role="baseline" / "candidate"  -> eligible to win (deterministic extractive).
7	  * role="yardstick_non_sovereign" -> ReaderLM-v2: benched for reference only,
8	    NEVER content-of-record, NEVER crowned (generative => fabrication risk +
9	    CC-BY-NC license). Enforced by `is_eligible_to_win` below, not a comment.
10	
11	needs_gpu candidates (MinerU-HTML, ReaderLM-v2) are gated behind a runtime check
12	in run_bakeoff.py: registered-but-skipped honestly when no GPU, never faked.
13	
14	Extractor callables raise `ExtractorLoadError` on import/load failure so the
15	GATE-0 liveness canary can distinguish a DEAD candidate (fail loud) from a
16	genuinely-low score on a hard page (a real result). They return the empty string
17	ONLY when the underlying tool genuinely produced nothing -- which the liveness
18	canary treats as a failure ON THE KNOWN-GOOD page.
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import os
24	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
25	from typing import Callable
26	
27	
28	class ExtractorLoadError(RuntimeError):
29	    """Raised fail-loud when an extractor cannot load/import (dead candidate)."""
30	
31	
32	@dataclass(frozen=True)
33	class Candidate:
34	    """A registered extractor candidate."""
35	
36	    name: str
37	    key: str  # short key matching PUBLISHED_ANCHORS where applicable
38	    impl_id: str  # exact pip/HF id (web-verified 2026-06-23)
39	    license: str
40	    role: str  # baseline | candidate | yardstick_non_sovereign
41	    needs_gpu: bool
42	    extractive: bool  # deterministic verbatim (True) vs generative (False)
43	    extract: Callable[[str], str] = field(repr=False)
44	
45	
46	def is_eligible_to_win(candidate: Candidate) -> bool:
47	    """STRUCTURAL never-crown: only extractive non-yardstick roles can win.
48	
49	    A higher ROUGE for ReaderLM-v2 can never crown it: this is checked by role,
50	    so the decision rule cannot be gamed by a bigger number.
51	    """
52	    return candidate.extractive and candidate.role != "yardstick_non_sovereign"
53	
54	
55	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	# Extractor implementations (lazy imports -> ExtractorLoadError on failure).
57	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
58	
59	
60	def _extract_trafilatura(html: str) -> str:
61	    """Production-faithful Trafilatura via the guarded POLARIS entrypoint.
62	
63	    Uses the prod kwargs (markdown, tables, no links) and the size-gate +
64	    SIGSEGV containment of safe_trafilatura_extract (access_bypass.py:943).
65	    """
66	    try:
67	        from src.tools.access_bypass import safe_trafilatura_extract
68	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 -- a missing seam is a dead candidate
69	        raise ExtractorLoadError(f"trafilatura seam import failed: {exc!r}") from exc
70	    out = safe_trafilatura_extract(
71	        html,
72	        output_format="markdown",
73	        include_tables=True,
74	        include_links=False,
75	    )
76	    return out or ""
77	
78	
79	def _extract_resiliparse(html: str) -> str:
80	    try:
81	        from resiliparse.extract.html2text import extract_plain_text  # type: ignore
82	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
83	        raise ExtractorLoadError(f"resiliparse import failed: {exc!r}") from exc
84	    return extract_plain_text(html, main_content=True) or ""
85	
86	
87	def _extract_justext(html: str) -> str:
88	    try:
89	        import justext  # type: ignore
90	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
91	        raise ExtractorLoadError(f"justext import failed: {exc!r}") from exc
92	    # MUST set English stoplist for the clinical fixture (raw-json note: its
93	    # published 0.4782 is partly a multilingual mis-set artifact).
94	    paragraphs = justext.justext(html, justext.get_stoplist("English"))
95	    return "\n".join(p.text for p in paragraphs if not p.is_boilerplate)
96	
97	
98	def _extract_readability(html: str) -> str:
99	    try:
100	        from readability import Document  # type: ignore  (readability-lxml)
101	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
102	        raise ExtractorLoadError(f"readability-lxml import failed: {exc!r}") from exc
103	    return Document(html).summary() or ""
104	
105	
106	def _extract_union(html: str) -> str:
107	    """Deterministic line-level union of trafilatura+resiliparse+justext.
108	
109	    Pure-extractive (every line comes verbatim from a member extractor) ->
110	    faithfulness-safe. Near-dup line collapse uses the named POLARIS
111	    ContentDeduplicator seam (seed-42 deterministic) rather than adding
112	    datasketch -- keeps determinism and avoids a new dependency.
113	    """
114	    members = [_extract_trafilatura(html), _extract_resiliparse(html), _extract_justext(html)]
115	    lines: list[str] = []
116	    seen_exact: set[str] = set()
117	    for body in members:
118	        for raw_line in (body or "").splitlines():
119	            line = raw_line.strip()
120	            if not line or line in seen_exact:
121	                continue
122	            seen_exact.add(line)
123	            lines.append(line)
124	    # Near-dup collapse across members (a line >=2 extractors emit slightly
125	    # differently) via the existing deterministic deduplicator.
126	    try:
127	        from src.utils.content_deduplicator import (
128	            ContentDeduplicator,
129	            DeduplicationConfig,
130	        )
131	
132	        dedup = ContentDeduplicator(
133	            DeduplicationConfig(near_duplicate_threshold=0.92, min_content_length=0)
134	        )
135	        items = [{"content": ln} for ln in lines]
136	        unique = dedup.get_unique_content(items)
137	        lines = [it["content"] for it in unique]
138	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 -- dedup is a refinement, not load-bearing;
139	        # if the seam is unavailable the exact-dup pass above already ran.
140	        pass
141	    return "\n".join(lines)
142	
143	
144	def _extract_mineru_html(html: str) -> str:
145	    """MinerU-HTML (opendatalab/MinerU-HTML, base Qwen3-0.6B) -- needs GPU/vLLM.
146	
147	    CONFIRMED EXTRACTIVE (Dripper paper: DOM-block label-and-reconstruct, no
148	    paraphrase). This callable is only invoked on a GPU host; on a CPU/smoke
149	    host the harness skips it via needs_gpu gating before calling.
150	    """
151	    try:
152	        from mineru_html import extract as mineru_extract  # type: ignore
153	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
154	        raise ExtractorLoadError(
155	            f"mineru-html import failed (needs_gpu; pip install .[baselines] "
156	            f"from github.com/opendatalab/MinerU-HTML): {exc!r}"
157	        ) from exc
158	    return mineru_extract(html) or ""
159	
160	
161	def _extract_readerlm_v2(html: str) -> str:
162	    """ReaderLM-v2 (jinaai/ReaderLM-v2) -- GENERATIVE yardstick, needs GPU.
163	
164	    Structurally barred from being crowned (role=yardstick_non_sovereign). The
165	    faithfulness substring check is EXPECTED to flag it -- that is the §-1.1
166	    anti-gaming demonstration, not a bug.
167	    """
168	    try:
169	        from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams  # type: ignore
170	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
171	        raise ExtractorLoadError(
172	            f"vLLM import failed for ReaderLM-v2 (needs_gpu): {exc!r}"
173	        ) from exc
174	    model_id = os.getenv("PG_READERLM_MODEL", "jinaai/ReaderLM-v2")
175	    llm = LLM(model=model_id)
176	    prompt = f"Extract the main content of this HTML as Markdown:\n{html}"
177	    result = llm.generate([prompt], SamplingParams(max_tokens=8192))
178	    return result[0].outputs[0].text if result else ""
179	
180	
181	def build_candidate_registry() -> list[Candidate]:
182	    """All extractor candidates, exact ids web-verified 2026-06-23."""
183	    return [
184	        Candidate(
185	            name="Trafilatura 2.x (incumbent content-of-record)",
186	            key="trafilatura",
187	            impl_id="pip: trafilatura==2.1.0",
188	            license="Apache-2.0",
189	            role="baseline",
190	            needs_gpu=False,
191	            extractive=True,
192	            extract=_extract_trafilatura,
193	        ),
194	        Candidate(
195	            name="Resiliparse (recall-biased ensemble partner)",
196	            key="resiliparse",
197	            impl_id="pip: chatnoir-resiliparse",
198	            license="Apache-2.0",
199	            role="candidate",
200	            needs_gpu=False,
201	            extractive=True,
202	            extract=_extract_resiliparse,
203	        ),
204	        Candidate(
205	            name="jusText (3rd union member)",
206	            key="justext",
207	            impl_id="pip: justext",
208	            license="Apache-2.0/BSD-2-Clause",
209	            role="candidate",
210	            needs_gpu=False,
211	            extractive=True,
212	            extract=_extract_justext,
213	        ),
214	        Candidate(
215	            name="readability-lxml (last-resort fallback baseline)",
216	            key="readability",
217	            impl_id="pip: readability-lxml==0.8.4.1",
218	            license="Apache-2.0",
219	            role="baseline",
220	            needs_gpu=False,
221	            extractive=True,
222	            extract=_extract_readability,
223	        ),
224	        Candidate(
225	            name="union(trafilatura+resiliparse+justext)",
226	            key="union",
227	            impl_id="composite (deterministic line-union + ContentDeduplicator collapse)",
228	            license="Apache-2.0 (union of Apache components)",
229	            role="candidate",
230	            needs_gpu=False,
231	            extractive=True,
232	            extract=_extract_union,
233	        ),
234	        Candidate(
235	            name="MinerU-HTML (Dripper SLM, clinical/table lane)",
236	            key="mineru_html",
237	            impl_id="HF: opendatalab/MinerU-HTML (v1.1, base Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B)",
238	            license="Apache-2.0",
239	            role="candidate",
240	            needs_gpu=True,
241	            extractive=True,
242	            extract=_extract_mineru_html,
243	        ),
244	        Candidate(
245	            name="ReaderLM-v2 (generative upper-bound yardstick)",
246	            key="readerlm_v2",
247	            impl_id="HF: jinaai/ReaderLM-v2",
248	            license="CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial -> YARDSTICK ONLY)",
249	            role="yardstick_non_sovereign",
250	            needs_gpu=True,
251	            extractive=False,
252	            extract=_extract_readerlm_v2,
253	        ),
254	    ]
255

1	"""Shared scoring + faithfulness primitives for the content_extraction bake-off.
2	
3	Two scorer paths kept strictly separate (advisor pt 3):
4	
5	  * SCORER-MATH canary path -- a pure-Python, tokenizer-agnostic ROUGE-N used by
6	    GATE-0's gold-in->~1.0 / junk-in->~0 sanity check and by the OFFLINE smoke.
7	    It needs NO network, NO jieba, NO model: it must run anywhere.
8	
9	  * PUBLISHED-NUMBER anchor path -- the WebMainBench OFFICIAL scorer
10	    (`eval_baselines.py`, jieba-tokenized ROUGE-N N=5) from the third-party
11	    `opendatalab/MinerU-HTML` repo. It is detect-or-fallback: used ONLY when the
12	    cloned repo is present; otherwise the documented blind re-derivation fires
13	    and is FLAGGED (never silently substituted, never circular).
14	
15	Plus the layer-specific FAITHFULNESS check: every content-of-record extractor
16	output span must be a verbatim substring of the fetched page's VISIBLE text
17	(advisor pt 2 -- markdown syntax stripped, whitespace normalized, checked at
18	content-span / table-cell granularity so extractive tools are not false-flagged
19	and a paraphrasing/generative path IS flagged). This is harness-internal; it
20	never invokes or relaxes the faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role /
21	provenance) -- those are not in this layer's data path at all.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import html as _html
27	import os
28	import re
29	import subprocess
30	import sys
31	import unicodedata
32	from dataclasses import dataclass
33	from typing import Iterable
34	
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	# Tunables (LAW VI: env-overridable, no magic numbers buried in logic).
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	
39	# Default n for the pure-Python scorer-math canary. The OFFICIAL anchor uses
40	# N=5 jieba (the paper's locked config); the canary is tokenizer-agnostic and
41	# small-N so it is meaningful on short synthetic fixtures.
42	SCORER_CANARY_ROUGE_N = int(os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_CANARY_ROUGE_N", "2"))
43	
44	# The OFFICIAL WebMainBench config (locked by the Dripper paper, arXiv 2511.23119).
45	OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N = int(os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N", "5"))
46	OFFICIAL_TOKENIZER = os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_OFFICIAL_TOKENIZER", "jieba")
47	
48	# GATE-0 thresholds for the scorer-math canary.
49	CANARY_PERFECT_MIN_F1 = float(os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_CANARY_PERFECT_MIN", "0.99"))
50	CANARY_JUNK_MAX_F1 = float(os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_CANARY_JUNK_MAX", "0.05"))
51	
52	# Faithfulness substring check: spans shorter than this many tokens are skipped
53	# (trivial fragments like "the" trivially substring-match and carry no signal).
54	FAITHFULNESS_MIN_SPAN_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_FAITH_MIN_TOKENS", "6"))
55	
56	# Fraction of (non-trivial) output spans that must be verbatim substrings of the
57	# source visible text for an extractor to PASS the faithfulness check. A truly
58	# extractive tool clears this comfortably; a generative rewrite does not.
59	FAITHFULNESS_MIN_VERBATIM_FRACTION = float(
60	    os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_FAITH_MIN_FRACTION", "0.90")
61	)
62	
63	
64	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	# Text normalization
66	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
67	
68	_WS_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
69	_HTML_TAG_RE = re.compile(r"<[^>]+>")
70	_HTML_SCRIPT_STYLE_RE = re.compile(
71	    r"<(script|style)\b[^>]*>.*?</\1>", re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
72	)
73	# Markdown syntax that extractors ADD and that is NOT present in source HTML text
74	# (so it must be stripped before the verbatim-substring check, advisor pt 2).
75	_MD_SYNTAX_RE = re.compile(r"(^[#>\-\*\+\|]+|[#\*`_~\|\[\]\(\)]+|\d+\.\s)")
76	
77	
78	def normalize_unicode(text: str) -> str:
79	    """NFKC-normalize so en-dashes/ligatures/width-variants match an extractor's
80	    decoded output (an extractive tool emits decoded, normalized text)."""
81	    return unicodedata.normalize("NFKC", text or "")
82	
83	
84	def normalize_whitespace(text: str) -> str:
85	    """Collapse all whitespace runs to single spaces; strip ends."""
86	    return _WS_RE.sub(" ", text or "").strip()
87	
88	
89	def html_visible_text(html: str) -> str:
90	    """Return the whitespace-normalized VISIBLE text of an HTML document.
91	
92	    Strips <script>/<style> bodies then all tags, then DECODES HTML entities
93	    (&   ' ...) and NFKC-normalizes Unicode. Without the decode +
94	    normalize, a faithful extractive tool's DECODED output would not be a
95	    substring of still-encoded source text -- a harness artifact that would
96	    wrongly hard-drop the lead candidate (advisor blocker 2). This is the
97	    reference surface the faithfulness check compares against.
98	    """
99	    if not html:
100	        return ""
101	    no_scripts = _HTML_SCRIPT_STYLE_RE.sub(" ", html)
102	    no_tags = _HTML_TAG_RE.sub(" ", no_scripts)
103	    decoded = _html.unescape(no_tags)
104	    return normalize_whitespace(normalize_unicode(decoded))
105	
106	
107	def strip_markdown_syntax(text: str) -> str:
108	    """Remove markdown decoration so output can be matched against plain text."""
109	    if not text:
110	        return ""
111	    stripped = _MD_SYNTAX_RE.sub(" ", text)
112	    return normalize_whitespace(stripped)
113	
114	
115	def tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]:
116	    """Whitespace + lowercase tokenization (tokenizer-agnostic canary path)."""
117	    return normalize_whitespace(text).lower().split()
118	
119	
120	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
121	# Pure-Python ROUGE-N (the SCORER-MATH canary path -- offline, no jieba)
122	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
123	
124	
125	@dataclass(frozen=True)
126	class RougeScore:
127	    """ROUGE-N decomposed into its reportable halves (sec_1_1 compliance)."""
128	
129	    n: int
130	    recall: float  # main-body completeness: did real gold content survive
131	    precision: float  # 1 - precision == junk leaked in
132	    f1: float
133	
134	    @property
135	    def junk_fraction(self) -> float:
136	        """Fraction of candidate n-grams absent from the gold body (chrome/SEO)."""
137	        return 1.0 - self.precision
138	
139	
140	def _ngrams(tokens: list[str], n: int) -> list[tuple[str, ...]]:
141	    if n <= 0 or len(tokens) < n:
142	        return []
143	    return [tuple(tokens[i : i + n]) for i in range(len(tokens) - n + 1)]
144	
145	
146	def _multiset_overlap(a: list[tuple], b: list[tuple]) -> int:
147	    """Clipped (multiset) overlap count -- the standard ROUGE counting rule."""
148	    from collections import Counter
149	
150	    ca, cb = Counter(a), Counter(b)
151	    return sum(min(ca[g], cb[g]) for g in ca.keys() & cb.keys())
152	
153	
154	def rouge_n(candidate: str, gold: str, n: int = SCORER_CANARY_ROUGE_N) -> RougeScore:
155	    """Pure-Python ROUGE-N F1 between candidate and gold (offline canary path).
156	
157	    recall    = overlap / |gold n-grams|       (completeness)
158	    precision = overlap / |candidate n-grams|  (1 - junk)
159	    f1        = harmonic mean
160	
161	    This is NOT the official jieba N=5 scorer (that is the published-number
162	    anchor path); this is the tokenizer-agnostic math used to PROVE the scorer
163	    rewards perfect extraction and scores junk low.
164	    """
165	    cand_tokens = tokenize(candidate)
166	    gold_tokens = tokenize(gold)
167	    cand_ng = _ngrams(cand_tokens, n)
168	    gold_ng = _ngrams(gold_tokens, n)
169	    if not cand_ng and not gold_ng:
170	        # Two empties: vacuously perfect (both nothing). Canary never feeds this.
171	        return RougeScore(n=n, recall=1.0, precision=1.0, f1=1.0)
172	    if not cand_ng or not gold_ng:
173	        return RougeScore(n=n, recall=0.0, precision=0.0, f1=0.0)
174	    overlap = _multiset_overlap(cand_ng, gold_ng)
175	    recall = overlap / len(gold_ng)
176	    precision = overlap / len(cand_ng)
177	    f1 = 0.0 if (recall + precision) == 0 else (2 * recall * precision) / (recall + precision)
178	    return RougeScore(n=n, recall=recall, precision=precision, f1=f1)
179	
180	
181	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
182	# FAITHFULNESS substring check (the layer-specific landmine, advisor pt 2)
183	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	
185	
186	@dataclass(frozen=True)
187	class FaithfulnessReport:
188	    """Result of the verbatim-substring faithfulness check on one extraction."""
189	
190	    checked_spans: int
191	    verbatim_spans: int
192	    verbatim_fraction: float
193	    is_faithful: bool
194	    first_violation: str = ""
195	
196	
197	def _content_spans(extractor_output: str) -> list[str]:
198	    """Split markdown-stripped output into checkable content spans.
199	
200	    Spans are sentence-ish / line-ish chunks; trivial fragments (< min tokens)
201	    are dropped so the fraction is computed over meaningful content only.
202	    """
203	    stripped = strip_markdown_syntax(extractor_output)
204	    # Split on sentence terminators AND newlines/pipes (table cells handled by
205	    # caller via check_table_cells); keep it deterministic and dependency-free.
206	    raw = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+|\n+|\s\|\s", stripped)
207	    spans: list[str] = []
208	    for chunk in raw:
209	        chunk = normalize_whitespace(chunk)
210	        if len(chunk.split()) >= FAITHFULNESS_MIN_SPAN_TOKENS:
211	            spans.append(chunk)
212	    return spans
213	
214	
215	def check_faithfulness(
216	    extractor_output: str,
217	    source_html: str,
218	    *,
219	    min_fraction: float = FAITHFULNESS_MIN_VERBATIM_FRACTION,
220	) -> FaithfulnessReport:
221	    """Assert extractor output spans are verbatim substrings of source visible text.
222	
223	    Extractive tools (Trafilatura, Resiliparse, jusText, readability, union,
224	    MinerU-HTML) clear this; a generative rewrite (ReaderLM-v2) does NOT, which
225	    is exactly the structural never-crown guard. Harness-internal: it does not
226	    touch the faithfulness engine.
227	    """
228	    source_text = html_visible_text(source_html).lower()
229	    spans = _content_spans(extractor_output)
230	    if not spans:
231	        # No checkable content -> cannot certify faithful (fail closed).
232	        return FaithfulnessReport(0, 0, 0.0, False, "no_checkable_spans")
233	    verbatim = 0
234	    first_violation = ""
235	    for span in spans:
236	        # Strip leading/trailing punctuation: an extractor adds a sentence-final
237	        # period that the source HTML omits at a block boundary (e.g. an <h1>
238	        # heading), so boundary punctuation must not cause a false violation.
239	        # NFKC-normalize so en-dashes / width-variants match the decoded source.
240	        # The WORDS are still required to be a contiguous verbatim substring --
241	        # a paraphrase still fails (advisor pt 2: must fail the paraphrase).
242	        probe = normalize_unicode(span).lower().strip(" .,;:!?—–-\"'()[]")
243	        if probe and probe in source_text:
244	            verbatim += 1
245	        elif not first_violation:
246	            first_violation = span[:160]
247	    fraction = verbatim / len(spans)
248	    return FaithfulnessReport(
249	        checked_spans=len(spans),
250	        verbatim_spans=verbatim,
251	        verbatim_fraction=fraction,
252	        is_faithful=fraction >= min_fraction,
253	        first_violation=first_violation,
254	    )
255	
256	
257	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
258	# Official WebMainBench scorer: DETECT-or-FALLBACK (advisor pt 4)
259	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
260	
261	
262	@dataclass(frozen=True)
263	class OfficialScorerStatus:
264	    """Whether the OFFICIAL WebMainBench scorer is present + runnable."""
265	
266	    available: bool
267	    eval_script_path: str
268	    benchmark_jsonl_path: str
269	    reason: str  # why available / why fell back
270	
271	
272	def locate_official_scorer(repo_root: str | None = None) -> OfficialScorerStatus:
273	    """Locate the cloned `opendatalab/MinerU-HTML` official scorer if present.
274	
275	    The scorer is `eval_baselines.py` + `benchmark/WebMainBench_100.jsonl`,
276	    cloned (not pip-installed, not vendored). If absent, the published-number
277	    GATE-0 anchor uses the DOCUMENTED blind re-derivation fallback and is
278	    FLAGGED -- never silently circular, never trusting a search-snippet number.
279	    """
280	    root = repo_root or os.getenv("PG_WEBMAINBENCH_REPO", "")
281	    if not root:
282	        return OfficialScorerStatus(
283	            available=False,
284	            eval_script_path="",
285	            benchmark_jsonl_path="",
286	            reason=(
287	                "PG_WEBMAINBENCH_REPO unset -- official eval_baselines.py not located; "
288	                "GATE-0 published-number anchor falls back to blind re-derivation (FLAGGED)."
289	            ),
290	        )
291	    eval_script = os.path.join(root, "eval_baselines.py")
292	    bench_jsonl = os.path.join(root, "benchmark", "WebMainBench_100.jsonl")
293	    if os.path.isfile(eval_script) and os.path.isfile(bench_jsonl):
294	        return OfficialScorerStatus(
295	            available=True,
296	            eval_script_path=eval_script,
297	            benchmark_jsonl_path=bench_jsonl,
298	            reason="official eval_baselines.py + WebMainBench_100.jsonl located",
299	        )
300	    return OfficialScorerStatus(
301	        available=False,
302	        eval_script_path=eval_script,
303	        benchmark_jsonl_path=bench_jsonl,
304	        reason=(
305	            f"PG_WEBMAINBENCH_REPO={root!r} present but eval_baselines.py and/or "
306	            "benchmark/WebMainBench_100.jsonl missing -- blind re-derivation fallback (FLAGGED)."
307	        ),
308	    )
309	
310	
311	def load_webmainbench_pages(status: OfficialScorerStatus, limit: int = 0) -> list[dict]:
312	    """Load the in-repo WebMainBench_100.jsonl pages (html + gold Markdown).
313	
314	    Returns [] when the official benchmark file is not located (the anchor
315	    reproduction is then honestly DEFERRED, never green-on-nothing). Each record
316	    exposes at least an 'html' field and a gold body field; the exact gold key
317	    is resolved leniently across the known WebMainBench schema variants.
318	    """
319	    import json
320	
321	    if not status.available or not os.path.isfile(status.benchmark_jsonl_path):
322	        return []
323	    pages: list[dict] = []
324	    with open(status.benchmark_jsonl_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
325	        for line in fh:
326	            line = line.strip()
327	            if not line:
328	                continue
329	            try:
330	                rec = json.loads(line)
331	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 -- skip a malformed line, never invent
332	                continue
333	            html = rec.get("html") or rec.get("raw_html") or ""
334	            gold = (
335	                rec.get("groundtruth_content")
336	                or rec.get("gold_markdown")
337	                or rec.get("markdown")
338	                or rec.get("content")
339	                or ""
340	            )
341	            if html and gold:
342	                pages.append({"html": html, "gold": gold})
343	            if limit and len(pages) >= limit:
344	                break
345	    return pages
346	
347	
348	def build_official_runner(status: OfficialScorerStatus):
349	    """Return a callable(candidate, gold)->f1 backed by the cloned official scorer.
350	
351	    Imports the official ROUGE-N (N=5, jieba) from the cloned MinerU-HTML repo on
352	    the host where it is present. Returns None when the repo or jieba is absent
353	    (so the caller falls back to the FLAGGED re-derivation). Kept here (not at
354	    module import) so this module stays offline-importable.
355	    """
356	    if not status.available:
357	        return None
358	    repo_root = os.path.dirname(status.eval_script_path)
359	    try:
360	        if repo_root not in sys.path:
361	            sys.path.insert(0, repo_root)
362	        import jieba  # type: ignore  # noqa: F401 -- the official tokenizer
363	        from webmainbench.metrics import rouge_n_f1  # type: ignore
364	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 -- repo layout/jieba missing -> flagged fallback
365	        return None
366	
367	    def _run(candidate: str, gold: str) -> float:
368	        # The official scorer's per-pair ROUGE-N F1 (N=5, jieba). Exact module
369	        # path is resolved at build time from the cloned repo; if its public API
370	        # name differs, this import fails above and the FLAGGED fallback fires.
371	        return float(rouge_n_f1(candidate, gold, n=OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N))
372	
373	    return _run
374	
375	
376	@dataclass(frozen=True)
377	class ScoredF1:
378	    """One F1 score plus WHICH scorer produced it (honest provenance)."""
379	
380	    f1: float
381	    recall: float
382	    precision: float
383	    scorer_used: str  # "official" | "fallback_rederived"
384	    detail: str = ""
385	
386	
387	def score_official_or_fallback(
388	    candidate_output: str,
389	    gold: str,
390	    status: OfficialScorerStatus,
391	    *,
392	    official_runner=None,
393	) -> ScoredF1:
394	    """PRIMARY = the OFFICIAL WebMainBench scorer (N=5 jieba) when located;
395	    else the pure-Python re-derivation at N=5, EXPLICITLY FLAGGED per result.
396	
397	    `official_runner` is an injectable callable(candidate_output, gold) -> f1
398	    that shells/imports the cloned `eval_baselines.py`; injected by run_bakeoff
399	    on the host where the repo is present (kept out of this module so the import
400	    surface stays offline-clean). When the official scorer is unavailable, the
401	    fallback runs at OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N (5) -- NOT the canary's small N -- and is
402	    recorded as scorer_used="fallback_rederived" so a downstream reader never
403	    mistakes a re-derived number for the published official one.
404	    """
405	    if status.available and official_runner is not None:
406	        try:
407	            f1 = float(official_runner(candidate_output, gold))
408	            return ScoredF1(
409	                f1=f1,
410	                recall=float("nan"),
411	                precision=float("nan"),
412	                scorer_used="official",
413	                detail=f"official eval_baselines.py (N={OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N}, {OFFICIAL_TOKENIZER})",
414	            )
415	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 -- a broken official run must be
416	            # visible (flagged), never silently swapped for the fallback as if equal.
417	            fb = rouge_n(candidate_output, gold, n=OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N)
418	            return ScoredF1(
419	                fb.f1,
420	                fb.recall,
421	                fb.precision,
422	                "fallback_rederived",
423	                f"official runner FAILED ({exc!r}); re-derived N={OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N} (FLAGGED)",
424	            )
425	    fb = rouge_n(candidate_output, gold, n=OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N)
426	    return ScoredF1(
427	        fb.f1,
428	        fb.recall,
429	        fb.precision,
430	        "fallback_rederived",
431	        f"official scorer unavailable; blind re-derivation N={OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N} (FLAGGED): {status.reason}",
432	    )
433	
434	
435	# Published Dripper Table-2 anchors, pinned from the repo's OWN results table
436	# (github.com/opendatalab/MinerU-HTML README + arXiv 2511.23119), NOT from a
437	# search snippet. Used ONLY as the tolerance target for the official-scorer
438	# reproduction check; never as a substitute for actually running the scorer.
439	# Each value is the WebMainBench_100 ROUGE-N (N=5, jieba) F1 for the named
440	# extractor variant. Tolerance band is intentionally wide (variant/split drift).
441	PUBLISHED_ANCHORS: dict[str, float] = {
442	    "trafilatura": 0.6402,
443	    "resiliparse": 0.6290,
444	    "readability": 0.6542,
445	    "justext": 0.4782,
446	    "mineru_html": 0.9001,
447	}
448	ANCHOR_TOLERANCE = float(os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_ANCHOR_TOL", "0.05"))
449	
450	
451	def anchor_within_tolerance(extractor_key: str, observed_f1: float) -> bool:
452	    """True iff observed official-scorer F1 matches the published anchor +/- tol."""
453	    if extractor_key not in PUBLISHED_ANCHORS:
454	        return False
455	    return abs(observed_f1 - PUBLISHED_ANCHORS[extractor_key]) <= ANCHOR_TOLERANCE
456	
457	
458	def average(values: Iterable[float]) -> float:
459	    vals = list(values)
460	    return sum(vals) / len(vals) if vals else 0.0

1	"""build_fixture.py -- ground truth for the content_extraction bake-off.
2	
3	Two ground-truth sources per the brief (§3 content_extraction):
4	
5	  1. GENERAL axis (REUSE, no build): WebMainBench -- 7,809 human-annotated pages
6	     with gold Markdown + the OFFICIAL ROUGE-N scorer (`eval_baselines.py` +
7	     `benchmark/WebMainBench_100.jsonl`, cloned from opendatalab/MinerU-HTML).
8	     This is the published gold for the exact job + the GATE-0 anchor. We do NOT
9	     re-annotate it; we load it (or honestly FLAG it absent).
10	
11	  2. CLINICAL axis (BUILD, needs_fixture): a small fixture (~50-100 real pages
12	     spanning POLARIS source types: journal HTML / FDA label / EMA SmPC /
13	     ClinicalTrials.gov / guideline / preprint) with gold main body Markdown +
14	     gold TABLE TREES (for TEDS). This module ships the SCHEMA + an ingest path
15	     that pulls raw bodies from banked corpus_snapshot.json files, but the GOLD
16	     LABELS are a human/two-family hand-labeling step -- they are NEVER
17	     fabricated. Until labeled, every clinical-axis verdict is honestly
18	     needs_fixture.
19	
20	Faithfulness: this builds reference data only; it never touches the faithfulness
21	engine. The labels are the metric's ground truth, set by humans, not by a judge
22	(a judge may PROPOSE a label; it never sets the scored label).
23	
24	Run:  python build_fixture.py --out outputs/ret_bakeoff/content_extraction
25	"""
26	
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import argparse
30	import glob
31	import hashlib
32	import json
33	import os
34	import sys
35	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
36	
37	from _scoring import locate_official_scorer
38	
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# Schema (LAW VI: paths/limits are args/env, not hardcoded)
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	
43	SCHEMA_VERSION = "content_extraction_fixture_v1"
44	
45	# POLARIS clinical source types the clinical fixture must span (oversample
46	# table-bearing pages). Used as a stratification target, not a hard filter.
47	CLINICAL_SOURCE_TYPES = (
48	    "journal_html",
49	    "fda_label",
50	    "ema_smpc",
51	    "clinicaltrials_gov",
52	    "clinical_guideline",
53	    "preprint",
54	)
55	
56	# Default banked snapshot search globs (the 6 banked corpora referenced across
57	# the campaign). Env-overridable; never assume a single hardcoded path.
58	DEFAULT_SNAPSHOT_GLOBS = (
59	    "outputs/**/corpus_snapshot.json",
60	    "state/**/corpus_snapshot*.json",
61	)
62	
63	
64	@dataclass
65	class ClinicalGoldPage:
66	    """One labeled clinical page. gold_* fields are HUMAN-labeled, never faked."""
67	
68	    page_id: str
69	    source_type: str  # one of CLINICAL_SOURCE_TYPES
70	    source_url: str
71	    raw_html: str  # the byte-identical fetched HTML (shared input, all candidates)
72	    has_table: bool
73	    # --- GOLD (human / two-family labeled; needs_fixture until filled) ---
74	    gold_main_body_md: str = ""  # gold main body Markdown
75	    gold_table_trees: list = field(default_factory=list)  # gold <table> HTML trees for TEDS
76	    label_status: str = "needs_fixture"  # needs_fixture | labeled
77	    label_provenance: str = ""  # who/what produced the label (human/two-family)
78	
79	
80	@dataclass
81	class GeneralAxisStatus:
82	    """WebMainBench reuse status (no build; detect-or-flag)."""
83	
84	    benchmark: str
85	    available: bool
86	    eval_script_path: str
87	    benchmark_jsonl_path: str
88	    reason: str
89	    gold_field: str = "groundtruth_content"  # WebMainBench gold key
90	    rouge_n: int = 5
91	    tokenizer: str = "jieba"
92	
93	
94	def _sha256_file(path: str) -> str:
95	    h = hashlib.sha256()
96	    with open(path, "rb") as fh:
97	        for block in iter(lambda: fh.read(65536), b""):
98	            h.update(block)
99	    return h.hexdigest()
100	
101	
102	def discover_snapshot_bodies(globs: tuple[str, ...], repo_root: str) -> list[dict]:
103	    """Ingest raw fetched bodies from banked corpus_snapshot.json files.
104	
105	    Returns candidate clinical-page stubs with raw HTML/body present but gold
106	    labels EMPTY (needs_fixture). This is the honest ingest path: real banked
107	    data in, no synthetic gold out.
108	    """
109	    stubs: list[dict] = []
110	    for pattern in globs:
111	        for snap_path in glob.glob(os.path.join(repo_root, pattern), recursive=True):
112	            try:
113	                with open(snap_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
114	                    snap = json.load(fh)
115	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 -- skip unreadable; never invent data
116	                continue
117	            sources = snap.get("sources") or snap.get("evidence") or []
118	            if not isinstance(sources, list):
119	                continue
120	            for idx, src in enumerate(sources):
121	                if not isinstance(src, dict):
122	                    continue
123	                raw = src.get("raw_html") or src.get("html") or src.get("content") or ""
124	                url = src.get("url") or src.get("source_url") or ""
125	                if not raw or not url:
126	                    continue
127	                stubs.append(
128	                    {
129	                        "page_id": f"{os.path.basename(snap_path)}#{idx}",
130	                        "source_url": url,
131	                        "raw_html": raw,
132	                        "snapshot": os.path.relpath(snap_path, repo_root),
133	                    }
134	                )
135	    return stubs
136	
137	
138	def build_general_axis_status(repo_root: str) -> GeneralAxisStatus:
139	    status = locate_official_scorer(os.getenv("PG_WEBMAINBENCH_REPO") or None)
140	    return GeneralAxisStatus(
141	        benchmark="WebMainBench (opendatalab/MinerU-HTML, 7809 pages, gold Markdown)",
142	        available=status.available,
143	        eval_script_path=status.eval_script_path,
144	        benchmark_jsonl_path=status.benchmark_jsonl_path,
145	        reason=status.reason,
146	    )
147	
148	
149	def build_fixture(
150	    *,
151	    out_dir: str,
152	    repo_root: str,
153	    snapshot_globs: tuple[str, ...],
154	    max_clinical_pages: int,
155	) -> dict:
156	    """Build/load both axes; write a manifest. Returns the manifest dict."""
157	    os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
158	
159	    general = build_general_axis_status(repo_root)
160	
161	    # Clinical axis: ingest real banked bodies as needs_fixture stubs. Gold
162	    # labels are NOT produced here (human/two-family step).
163	    stubs = discover_snapshot_bodies(snapshot_globs, repo_root)
164	    clinical_pages = [
165	        ClinicalGoldPage(
166	            page_id=s["page_id"],
167	            source_type="unclassified",  # human labels the type during annotation
168	            source_url=s["source_url"],
169	            raw_html=s["raw_html"],
170	            has_table="<table" in (s["raw_html"] or "").lower(),
171	            label_status="needs_fixture",
172	            label_provenance=f"ingested_from:{s['snapshot']}",
173	        )
174	        for s in stubs[:max_clinical_pages]
175	    ]
176	
177	    clinical_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "clinical_gold_fixture.jsonl")
178	    with open(clinical_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
179	        for page in clinical_pages:
180	            fh.write(json.dumps(asdict(page), ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
181	
182	    manifest = {
183	        "schema_version": SCHEMA_VERSION,
184	        "general_axis": asdict(general),
185	        "clinical_axis": {
186	            "fixture_path": os.path.relpath(clinical_path, repo_root),
187	            "page_count": len(clinical_pages),
188	            "labeled_count": sum(1 for p in clinical_pages if p.label_status == "labeled"),
189	            "needs_fixture_count": sum(
190	                1 for p in clinical_pages if p.label_status == "needs_fixture"
191	            ),
192	            "source_types_targeted": list(CLINICAL_SOURCE_TYPES),
193	            "table_bearing_count": sum(1 for p in clinical_pages if p.has_table),
194	            "gold_labeling": (
195	                "HUMAN / two-family hand-labeling required (gold body + table trees). "
196	                "Judge may PROPOSE; never sets the scored label. needs_fixture until labeled."
197	            ),
198	        },
199	        "fixture_sha256": _sha256_file(clinical_path),
200	    }
201	    with open(os.path.join(out_dir, "fixture_manifest.json"), "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
202	        json.dump(manifest, fh, indent=2)
203	    return manifest
204	
205	
206	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
207	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Build content_extraction bake-off fixtures")
208	    parser.add_argument(
209	        "--out",
210	        default=os.getenv(
211	            "PG_CE_BAKEOFF_OUT", "outputs/ret_bakeoff/content_extraction"
212	        ),
213	    )
214	    parser.add_argument("--repo-root", default=os.getenv("PG_REPO_ROOT", os.getcwd()))
215	    parser.add_argument(
216	        "--max-clinical-pages",
217	        type=int,
218	        default=int(os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_MAX_CLINICAL", "100")),
219	    )
220	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
221	
222	    manifest = build_fixture(
223	        out_dir=args.out,
224	        repo_root=args.repo_root,
225	        snapshot_globs=DEFAULT_SNAPSHOT_GLOBS,
226	        max_clinical_pages=args.max_clinical_pages,
227	    )
228	    print(json.dumps(manifest, indent=2))
229	    if not manifest["general_axis"]["available"]:
230	        print(
231	            "[FLAG] WebMainBench official scorer NOT located -- GATE-0 published-number "
232	            "anchor will use blind re-derivation fallback (set PG_WEBMAINBENCH_REPO).",
233	            file=sys.stderr,
234	        )
235	    if manifest["clinical_axis"]["labeled_count"] == 0:
236	        print(
237	            "[FLAG] clinical fixture is needs_fixture (0 labeled) -- clinical-axis "
238	            "(TEDS / in-domain) verdicts are NOT trusted until human/two-family labeling.",
239	            file=sys.stderr,
240	        )
241	    return 0
242	
243	
244	if __name__ == "__main__":
245	    raise SystemExit(main())
